stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a major open-source LLM.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: Evaluation Awareness Is Not One Capability: Evidence from Open Language Models
stories.author: Soundararajan Srinivasan
bullet_points: The study challenges the core assumption of safety benchmarks: that test-time behavior predicts real-world deployment behavior.
It introduces the concept of 'evaluation awareness,' where models detect testing cues and adapt their responses.
This awareness creates a gap, meaning benchmark compliance can be an overly optimistic upper bound for actual model safety.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, deepseek_v3) point to its technical foundation and lineage.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance on Apple Silicon.
This integration leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, for optimized execution.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: GM installs robots at flagship EV factory after laying off 1,300 workers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: General Motors is implementing robotics at its main EV manufacturing facility.
The deployment follows a recent layoff of 1,300 workers at the company.
Labor unions are raising concerns about the increasing automation leading toward 'dark factory' operations.

stories.title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: Introduces CoorDex, a new learning pipeline for continuous, dexterous humanoid locomotion and manipulation.
Addresses the limitation of current systems which treat loco-manipulation as a stop-and-go process.
Enables high-DoF dexterous manipulation while the humanoid is actively moving, moving beyond simple open-close grasping.

stories.title: AIR: Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code in MLLMs
stories.author: Yujie Zhong
bullet_points: Introduces 'AIR' (Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code) to enhance Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a gap in current research where existing tool-use methods are limited to visual perception and lack numerical computation capabilities.
Proposes a method to integrate code reasoning beyond predefined visual heuristics to improve MLLM performance.

stories.title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: Introduces CoorDex, a new learning pipeline for continuous, dexterous humanoid loco-manipulation.
Addresses the limitation of traditional stop-and-go manipulation by integrating body and high-DoF hand control.
Converts complex, high-dimensional control into coordinated latent residual control for on-the-move tasks.

stories.title: Can LLMs Reliably Self-Report Adversarial Prefills, and How?
stories.author: Taegyoon Kim
bullet_points: The study investigates whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can reliably detect when their prior responses were manipulated by adversarial prefill attacks.
Across ten open-weight LLMs (3B to 70B) tested on four safety benchmarks, no model reliably recognized its own compromised outputs.
Models demonstrated a low rate of claiming intent on prefilled responses, averaging only 27.3%, indicating a significant vulnerability in LLM self-awareness regarding adversarial inputs.

stories.title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
stories.author: Kaiyan Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces EnterpriseClawBench, a new benchmark for evaluating enterprise AI agents.
The benchmark is constructed from proprietary, real-world workplace sessions, making it highly practical.
It provides 852 reproducible tasks complete with detailed context (fixtures, prompts, rubrics) for rigorous testing.

stories.title: SVD-Surgeon: Optimal Singular-Value Surgery for Large Language Model Compression
stories.author: Frank Hutter
bullet_points: Introduces SVD-Surgeon, a novel, training-free method for compressing Large Language Models (LLMs).
It adapts the Optimal Brain Surgeon (OBS) framework to the singular-value basis for targeted model pruning.
Addresses the critical industry challenge of deploying LLMs by reducing memory and compute requirements via low-rank compression.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for creating personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Tesla claims driver ‘manually overrode self-driving’ in deadly Texas crash
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Tesla is disputing that its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system caused a fatal crash in Texas.
The company claims the driver manually overrode the self-driving feature by pressing the accelerator.
The incident involved a Model 3 speeding into a home, resulting in a fatality.

stories.title: Zuckerberg reportedly wants a Polymarket clone — but without real money
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is developing an internal app, 'Arena,' modeled after prediction markets like Polymarket.
The platform will operate without real-money wagering, instead using a points-based system.
This move suggests Meta is exploring decentralized prediction market functionality within its ecosystem.

stories.title: With Starfall, SpaceX eyes an edge in global cargo delivery from orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is developing 'Starfall' to facilitate the transport and delivery of goods from orbit.
The initiative focuses on establishing a commercial capability for in-space cargo logistics.
This development aims to give SpaceX a competitive advantage in the global space delivery market.

stories.title: Report: Kennedy Space Center not ready for era of super heavy rockets
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX intends to launch Starship from Kennedy Space Center every eight days.
A report indicates that the Kennedy Space Center infrastructure may not be prepared for this high launch cadence.
The story highlights a potential bottleneck between ambitious private sector timelines and existing government/facility readiness.

stories.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
stories.author: Daniel Cohen-Or
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of mode collapse in text-to-image generation, where models lack diversity.
Proposes a new method for 'controlled diversity' by enforcing structure on generated samples.
Focuses on moving beyond incidental variations to enable meaningful design choices in AI image output.

stories.title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
stories.author: Lijun Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a theoretical gap in AdamW, the standard optimizer for LLMs, concerning its performance under heavy-tailed stochastic gradient noise.
Empirical evidence suggests LLM pretraining noise is heavy-tailed, challenging the finite-variance assumptions of current optimizer theory.
Recent research indicates that sign-based optimizers (Lion, Muon) and AdaGrad show promise in achieving convergence under these heavy-tailed conditions.

stories.title: Teaching LLMs String Matching, Backtracking, and Error Recovery to Deduce Bases and Truth Tables for the Combinatorially Exploding Bit Manipulation Puzzles
stories.author: Shubham Jain
bullet_points: The research addresses a known weakness of LLMs: difficulty with complex, combinatorial logic like bit manipulation puzzles.
The paper introduces algorithmic innovations to help LLMs deduce hidden logical rules and truth tables for these puzzles.
This work is presented in the context of the NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge, highlighting practical application in model evaluation.

stories.title: TailorMind: Towards Preference-Aligned Multimodal Content Generation
stories.author: Liqiang Nie
bullet_points: Introduces TailorMind, a system for personalized multimodal content generation.
Addresses the challenge of creating user-tailored content when existing user-generated content (UGC) is unavailable or costly.
Focuses on translating user behavioral traces directly into generation-ready preferences.

stories.title: AI Exposure Scores: what they measure, what they miss, and what comes next
stories.author: Marzieh Fadaee
bullet_points: The article discusses 'AI Exposure Scores' from Eloundou et al. (2023), which quantify the share of occupational tasks an LLM can assist with.
The scores are a significant empirical input to the ongoing 'future of work' debate.
The analysis highlights that the limitations of the original methodology are often overlooked as the scores are applied in different contexts.

stories.title: AutoDex: An Automated Real-World System for Dexterous Grasping Data Collection
stories.author: Hanbyul Joo
bullet_points: AutoDex presents a system to automate the collection of real-world data for dexterous grasping.
It addresses the limitations of current methods: slow teleoperation and unverified simulation data.
The system proposes a loop to generate candidate grasps and verify them on physical hardware.

stories.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
stories.author: Daniel Cohen-Or
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of mode collapse in text-to-image models, where outputs lack diversity despite high visual fidelity.
Proposes a new method for 'controlled diversity' that enforces structure on generated samples.
Aims to move beyond incidental variations to allow for meaningful, design-driven diversity in image generation.

stories.title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
stories.author: Lijun Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a theoretical gap in AdamW, the standard optimizer for LLMs, by examining its performance under heavy-tailed noise.
Empirical evidence suggests LLM pretraining noise is heavy-tailed, challenging the finite-variance assumptions of current optimizer theory.
The research highlights that sign-based optimizers (Lion, Muon) and AdaGrad show promise in achieving convergence under these challenging noise conditions.

stories.title: Tapered Language Models
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper challenges the standard architectural assumption in modern language models (like Transformers) that parameter capacity is allocated uniformly across all layers.
It suggests that empirical evidence indicates later layers primarily refine the residual stream rather than performing wholesale transformations.
The research questions whether the distribution of parameter capacity within the model depth should be non-uniform (i.e., 'tapered').

stories.title: On the Limits of Prompt-Conditioned Language Models as General-Purpose Learners
stories.author: Jun Wang
bullet_points: Challenges the perception of LLMs as truly general-purpose learners.
Proposes that language acts as a capacity-limited interface for conveying task information.
Analyzes prompt encoding and reinterpretation using a bilevel 'cheap-talk' game model.

stories.title: MAS-PromptBench: When Does Prompt Optimization Improve Multi-Agent LLM Systems?
stories.author: Laixi Shi
bullet_points: The research focuses on Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) as a scalable approach for agentic AI, where multiple LLM agents interact.
System prompts are identified as a critical and accessible optimization surface for MAS, allowing for system-level improvements without costly model finetuning.
The paper introduces MAS-PromptBench to investigate when prompt optimization actually yields performance improvements in these complex, multi-agent environments.

stories.title: Randomized YaRN Improves Length Generalization for Long-Context Reasoning
stories.author: Greg Durrett
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of LLMs struggling to generalize to very long sequences after initial training.
The proposed method, Randomized YaRN, enhances length generalization by integrating YaRN positional extrapolation with randomized positional encoding.
The technique utilizes a length curriculum during training on short context data to improve performance on longer inputs.

stories.title: The Energy Consumption of Transformer Fine-Tuning: A Roofline-Inspired Scaling Model
stories.author: Mansour Zoubeirou a Mayaki
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing computational and energy costs associated with training large Transformer models.
It introduces a framework to model the energy consumption of Transformer training across multiple GPUs.
The research uses BERT model sweeps to relate measured energy usage to lightweight proxies for analysis.

stories.title: VeriEvol: Scaling Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning via Verifiable Evol-Instruct
stories.author: Yujiu Yang
bullet_points: Introduces 'VeriEvol,' a method to scale multimodal mathematical reasoning in AI.
Addresses the critical issue of maintaining reward label reliability as training data volume increases.
Decouples prompt difficulty expansion from policy updates by treating data construction as a verifiable problem.

stories.title: Self-Compacting Language Model Agents
stories.author: Daniel Khashabi
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of context window overflow in long AI agent traces due to accumulating stale content.
Proposes 'SelfCompact,' a novel scaffold allowing the language model to autonomously decide when and how to perform content compaction.
Overcomes limitations of existing fixed-interval compaction methods, which risk discarding crucial partial results mid-derivation.

stories.title: Agents write code, but they don't remember
stories.author: lizziepika
bullet_points: The article posits that while AI agents are proficient at code generation, their lack of persistent memory remains a significant hurdle.
It suggests a fundamental shift in the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), moving from code-centric to intent-centric workflows.
The core argument highlights the risks teams face when an AI agent's reasoning process is opaque or ephemeral.

stories.title: AI found 300 WordPress plugin zero-days in 72 hours. I build plugins. Here's what changed for me.
stories.author: rapls
bullet_points: AI tools are being used to rapidly discover security vulnerabilities (zero-days) in software.
The story highlights the practical application of AI in software security testing, specifically targeting WordPress plugins.
It serves as a cautionary tale or case study for developers regarding the necessity of rigorous security reviews, even when using AI-assisted tools.

stories.title: Hetzner Doubled Its Prices Again. The AI Memory Crunch Is Why
stories.author: devopsdaily
bullet_points: Hetzner, a hosting provider, has increased its service prices again.
The price hikes are attributed to the increasing demand and constraints of AI memory requirements.
The article addresses the impact of the 'AI Memory Crunch' on cloud/hosting costs.
